amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Pre-mRNA splicing in S. cerevisiae removes introns via the spliceosome, a megadalton ribonucleoprotein complex. The spliceosome assembles stepwise on pre-mRNA: U1 snRNP recognizes 5' splice site, U2 snRNP binds branch point, U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP joins to form catalytic core. After extensive remodeling driven by eight DExD/H-box ATPases/helicases, two transesterification reactions occur: branch point attacks 5' splice site (forming lariat), then 3'OH of 5' exon attacks 3' splice site (joining exons, releasing lariat). Nobel Prize to Sharp and Roberts.

name: Pre-mRNA Splicing by the Spliceosome
notes: RNA splicing has VERY high AND gates (12) - all snRNPs and sequential steps required. Nobel Prize work.
scientificAccuracy: Based on Nobel Prize-winning research on splicing mechanisms. All spliceosome components validated.

sources.title: Mechanical devices of the spliceosome: motors, clocks, springs, and levers
Structure of a yeast step I spliceosome at 3.6-Å resolution
sources.authors: Yan, C., Hang, J., Wan, R., Huang, X., Wong, CC., Shi, Y.

sources.title: Structure of a yeast step II catalytically activated spliceosome
